title: Advice For Entrepreneurs | #shorts
description:
Divya Gugnani taught me to fight for my business every single day.

Every single day.

I remember when I was having a moment and time of feeling self-pity she called me and said you think Hollywood is thinking let us try to find an Indian woman sitting somewhere in Manhattan on the Upper East Side and let us dig her out?

No! you have to throw yourself at them every day. Stop the self pity, stop the shame and get out there and fight like every other entrepreneur has to fight for their business.

Divya is a case study in relentlessness, innovation, hustle, strategy, and paying it forward. I’ve seen her take people under her ring that honestly don’t even deserve the shot, but she tries so hard. God bless her.

We need more Divya Gugnanis in the world - everyone needs one and I’m so grateful to have my own.

title: NY Times Bestseller!!! | #shorts
description:
NY Times bestseller in 2 categories!!!!
USA Today bestseller in overall book sales competing with *all* books sold in America
Amazon bestseller

Thank you to everyone who pre-ordered,who wrote a review, who shared word of our book, who bought multiple copies to read with their sisters with their uncle’s auntie‘s brothers, for book clubs who added us to their must-read list, to business schools who added us to their curriculums to business institutions who decided to support us to all my famous friends who stepped up in the biggest way possible to be a part of my story - I wrote this book straight out of my heart with my unfinished half broken english, but with faith that if I wrote something real, my audience will understand and resonate with it and boy did you guys come through!

Huge huge thanks to @zoyagarg_ who went from not knowing what a book pre-order was to running executing and delivering a flawless 10,000+ book pre-order campaign, an honest campaign built by pounding the pavement, every single day, lobbying, persuading, selling informing, sometimes bullying and all out making it happen for her mom.

I told Zoya that if she wants to work with me, she will have to start with doing the hardest absolutely most difficult job first otherwise there’s no job for her in my business and she not only delivered in spades, but then didn’t stop until she started getting reviews because we were informed that reviews were equally as important that we wanted the world to share how they were feeling while reading this book and to share their feelings, so our book could spread its message of love and positivity all over the world.

Tomorrow Zoya will be in search of something new to cry about so stay tuned …

Come for the book, stay for the drama. This is not your average book launch.

title: Why I Wrote this Book
description:
I wrote this book through tears, laughter, and sheer willpower, because I had to. I had to tell the truth about what it takes to start over, to chase a dream when it feels too late, and to believe in yourself when no one else does.

I wrote it for the women who feel stuck. For the stay-at-home moms who wonder if they’re allowed to want more. For the daughters who dream with no roadmap. For my own children, so they’ll never doubt what’s possible.

I pray that everyone, in whatever chapter they’re in, finds a way to a life they love.

And if this story helps even one person feel less alone or more seen… it was worth every word.

#ThisAmericanWoman 💛

title: The Zarna Garg Family Podcast | Ep. 62: Is American Politics Just Corrupt?
description:
It’s no secret what many of you think about American politics—corruption runs deep, politicians put their own families at risk, and most people have no idea what their local representatives even do. They question why only a certain educational degree seems to ‘qualify’ someone for office when intelligence isn’t limited to one field. Today, the Gargs unravel the scary realities of American politics and navigate the challenge of keeping the family intact with clashing political views.

description:

The Gargs don’t believe in gatekeeping success, so we’re sharing how the government incentivizes entrepreneurship and the true rewards of working for yourself. But we’re also tackling the loneliness of being your own boss and navigating the stark divide between corporate life and entrepreneurship. So, is the risk worth it?

title: The Zarna Garg Family Podcast | Ep. 38: Education Redesigned
description:
What if you could redesign the entire education system from scratch? 

Veer maps out the perfect school, Brij advocates for financial literacy curriculum, a fitness center, and overpaid teachers, and Zarna begs to scrap humanities at large (despite her secret passion for writing). From carpentry to driving school, practical skills take center stage, but the real fun begins when each family member gets to make three outrageous wishes from a genie.

description:
Ever wondered how financial wisdom can transform your life? Join us while my husband, who's been knee-deep in finance for 25 years, reveals the glamorous secrets of patience in career growth and the heart-pounding excitement of low-fee index funds. Spoiler: it’s like watching paint dry, but with money! The kids hone in on what financial independence mans to them, and perhaps we even share tips that might just make you a millionaire—or at least help you survive the next rent cycle.

title: The Zarna Garg Family Podcast | Ep. 33: Charge Your Kids Rent!
description:
Ever considered charging your kids rent?

Financial independence is not just a personal milestone - it’s a socio-cultural challenge, especially within immigrant families. Tune in while our kids open up about their take on parental control over finances and the pressures of becoming self-sufficient. We tackle the practicality of shared expenses, the nuances of prenups, and the cultural expectations of caring for parents in their later years. But most importantly, why kids should be paying their dues through RENT.

title: The Zarna Garg Family Podcast | Ep. 32: Coffee Over Cocktails
description:

title: Cutting Chai Recipe
description:
No cinnamon, no cardamom, this chai is not for light weights - this is the chai that laborers/blue collar workers in India drink to keep their spirits high and to stay in top shape.
.
Even though I was born in affluence (lost it all, but still), I hated all the fluffy, over-spiced tea and instead I would seek this tea out everywhere - my kids, friends, family love it and I made this rambling tutorial in the spirit of sharing how I make it - I can’t believe all the places this video has reached - thank you for laughing and learning with me 🙏🏾♥️

title: The Beginning #shorts
description:
Where it all started and where I return to get ready for round 2 - after months of mind-bending, neurotic hyper-perfecting every word of a joke prepping to film a special, I was back to riffing for fun - I can’t believe how much I enjoyed it and the audience played along!
.
Thank you @westsidecomedyclub for being such a warm spot always - miserable Mrs Madison, Felicia Madison, created this oasis of joy despite her best efforts stay on brand 😂 and thank you @our.salon on IG for these photos - you rally captured a vibe♥️
.
#indiancomics #standupcomedy #womenincomedy #zarnagarg

title: Tamron Hall Show!
description:
What an honor to have been featured on Tamron’s Golden Comedy Hour with the one and only Tamron Hall, alongside my daughter Zoya Garg. Look at how far we’ve come! Thank you to everyone, yes, all of YOU, for your support since day 1! It’s only going up from here! 

#tamron #tamronhallshow #news #tvpresence #zarna #zarnagarg #indianlife #india #comedy #jokes #family #kids #womenincomedy #browncomedy

title: This American Life with Ira Glass and Zoya Garg
description:
Youtube-fam!  Check out this week’s episode of @this-american-life with Ira Glass - it’s titled ‘Ends of the Earth’ - An exploration of the very upper limits of what you do for someone you love. 
.
Two contrasting stories that are real, raw and told in a way that only Ira Glass can.  You must check them both out.
.
An audio excerpt from my story in Act 2 is reproduced with permission from the show - you can hear the whole episode on their webpage thisamericanlife.org
.
Warning: Once you hear one, you’re going to want to binge all! 
.
Thank you This American Life for featuring Zoya Garg and 🙋🏽‍♀️ - we had the best time and beans were spilled!
.
#thisamericanlife #podcast #podcastlife #indiancomics #standupcomedy #iraglass #zarnagarg

title: Closing Bell at Nasdaq! #shorts
description:
Closing bell Nasdaq today with @CarolinesOnBroadway  and @TimesSquareTSq  

We’ve performed at many outdoor shows this year as a community-building exercise organized by Caroline's on Broadway and Times Square NYC because we are New Yorker's and we will FIGHT to keep our city 💯 

Thank you Nasdaq, Caroline's on Broadway, and Times Square NYC for including me and giving me an opportunity to be of service to my home of 24 years - there’s no place like it 🗽

title: Zarna Garg Live At The Laugh Factory Chicago
description:
Together we celebrate being Indian, being immigrants, being moms, being people who truly love diversity, being funny and deep at the same time, being pained and happy in the waves - my shows celebrate us being one

San Diego, San Fransisco, Brunswick, Bridgeport, Aruba, Nantucket, and more coming soon! 

Join us for a live experience in a city near you - sign up for my newsletter zarnagarg.com to stay on top of it!

title: Indian Immigrant Comedian Takes the Stage
description:
Zarna Garg, the WINNER OF KEVIN HART'S LYFT COMICS, WINNER OF LADIES OF LAUGHTER 2021, WINNER OF TOP COMEDY FEATURE AUSTIN FILM FESTIVAL AND MUCH MORE, is one in a billion! 

As a regular at the iconic Comedy Cellar and Carolines on Broadway in NYC, she has also performed in Vegas and the famed Kennedy Center, and is touring nationwide in 2022. More popular than her teen children (as expected), she is a TikTok comedy sensation with over 80 million views and 439k followers! 

Stay tuned for more content!

description:
